summary: Verified BOTH tiny UI changes end-to-end. CHANGE #1: CreateFamilyModal now shows only 3 elements (name input, language select, submit button); zero <input type='file'> present in modal; no 'cover photo' text; POST /api/families succeeds and new family renders on dashboard. CHANGE #2: The 4th sidebar item reads exactly 'Stories & Traditions' in desktop sidebar, mobile drawer, and mobile bottom nav; /app/stories page H1 reads 'Stories & Traditions'. Regression: MarketingHeader still renders plain 'Stories' link to /stories (rename correctly scoped to authenticated app only). No console errors caused by removed ImageIcon import (only expected 401 from /api/auth/me on unauthenticated demo session).
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx CreateFamilyModal cleanly removes ImageIcon import + handleFile + cover state; no dead code remains. All three data-testids (create-family-name, create-family-language, create-family-submit) are correctly attached.
mock.js sidebarLinks label change propagates to all three renders (desktop sidebar, mobile drawer, mobile bottom nav) via AppShell.jsx sidebarLinks.slice(...) — verified visually and via DOM query.
PublicCookbook.jsx still references family.coverPhoto for historical families that previously had one — regression-safe.
MarketingHeader.jsx and MarketingFooter.jsx retain 'Stories' label (as intended) — scope of rename is correct.
test_credentials: None used — demo user flow (Meera R.) via /app route works without login.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test family 'TESTFAM_1784869748' via CreateFamilyModal (verified POST /api/families works); deleted after test via DELETE /api/family/{id} (HTTP 200 {ok:true}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both micro-changes shipped correctly. If regression sweeps needed, sidebarLinks label lives in /app/frontend/src/mock.js line 74 and Stories page H1 in /app/frontend/src/pages/app/StoriesPage.jsx line 42. CreateFamilyModal is in Dashboard.jsx around line 271-308. Marketing pages intentionally keep 'Stories'.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found; both changes verified as implemented correctly.
